stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
stories.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal with Chelsea for 22-year-old winger Alejandro Garnacho, with an obligation to buy. The deal is for one year, with the option to make it permanent next summer if certain conditions are met. Chelsea will receive £43m if the deal becomes permanent. Garnacho has already completed a medical and is set to sign a four-year contract at Villa Park.

stories.title: French modeling scout with links to Epstein found dead
stories.summary: French modeling scout Daniel Siad, 69, with ties to convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been found dead at his home outside Paris. Siad was under investigation in France over allegations of aiding Epstein to traffic and abuse women, and was accused of rape and human trafficking by at least five women. An autopsy will be carried out to determine the cause of death, which authorities have not yet confirmed. The investigation into Siad's alleged crimes will continue despite his death.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 MK Dons: Top 10 talking points
stories.summary: Tottenham Hotspur began their preseason campaign with a 1-0 win over MK Dons in a behind-closed-doors friendly at their training ground. Mateus Fernandes scored the lone goal with a stunning strike, marking his debut along with new signings Sandro Tonali and Martin Dubravka. The match saw a mix of youth and experience, with several players missing due to the World Cup. Tottenham will look to build on this momentum as they continue their preseason training.

stories.title: Rosalía breaks her silence after sparking outrage among Argentine fans: “My bad, sorry."
stories.summary: Spanish singer Rosalía has apologized for sparking outrage among Argentine fans after she reposted a social media video that many viewed as a jab at their national team. The incident occurred during the World Cup. Rosalía deleted the post and issued a brief apology, stating she had shared the video without reading its caption or understanding the message. Her apology comes as criticism spread online.

stories.title: Aston Villa Launch Loan Bid For £42.5 Outcast: Is He Needed For Unai Emery?
stories.summary: Aston Villa has launched a loan bid for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho, with a £42.5 million price tag attached to a permanent transfer. The 22-year-old Argentine international will join Villa Park on loan with a buy clause that will become active once certain conditions are met. Garnacho's departure from Chelsea marks the end of a frustrating first campaign in west London, where he netted once and provided four assists in 24 Premier League games. Unai Emery's interest in the winger is not new, and a medical exam has been planned for the incoming player.

stories.title: Unai Emery Convinces Aston Villa to Complete Move for Alejandro Garnacho: Reports
stories.summary: Aston Villa have agreed to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea on loan with a conditional buy clause. The 22-year-old Argentina winger will undergo a medical before finalizing the move. Villa’s interest follows Unai Emery’s pursuit, with Chelsea set to receive around £42.5 million if conditions are met. The deal comes as Garnacho’s first season at Stamford Bridge fell short, producing just one goal and four assists in 24 Premier League appearances.

stories.title: Romano: Summerville accepts Al Hilal’s offer
stories.summary: Crysencio Summerville has accepted Al Hilal's offer to join the Saudi Pro League club, with a base fee of £55 million and potential add-ons of £10 million. The 24-year-old Dutch winger is on his way to Saudi to undergo a medical examination. This move represents a significant profit for West Ham United, who signed Summerville in 2024. Al Hilal's ambitious bid has secured the signing, with Summerville set to strengthen the Saudi club's squad.

stories.title: Peter Lim visita Valencia para ver en persona a Inma Ibáñez y supervisar el progreso del Nou Mestalla
stories.summary: Valencia CF's owner Peter Lim has returned to Valencia, Spain, after attending the World Cup final in the US, where Spain won its second star. Lim visited the Nou Mestalla stadium, the future home of Valencia CF, accompanied by top club officials. The stadium is in a new phase of construction after the installation of a compression ring and 50 steel pillars. Lim also met with Inma Ibáñez, the club's director of the foundation, and discussed the club's summer market situation.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 LIVE RESULTS: Play SUSPENDED during Menzies' match
stories.summary: Play at the World Matchplay in Blackpool was suspended due to Cameron Menzies' apparent illness, forcing his withdrawal from the tournament. Menzies was leading Ross Smith 2-1 when he slumped over and was helped off the stage. He has been cleared by medics but will not return to continue his match. Smith has progressed to the quarter-finals as a result. The night's second match, Gian van Veen vs Wessel Nijman, has been brought forward.

stories.title: El Supremo admite el recurso para parar las altas en el censo por la Ley de Nietos y pide audiencia a la JEC
stories.summary: The Spanish Supreme Court has admitted a lawsuit to halt the implementation of the "Law of Grandchildren" in the foreign voter census, following a complaint by the party Iustitia Europa. The law allows grandchildren of Spanish citizens to vote, even if they have never lived in Spain. The court has given the Electoral Central Board 10 days to respond to the suspension request. The case is set to be heard in September, with potential implications for the 2.5 million people who have applied for Spanish citizenship.

stories.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
stories.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas have welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, on July 21. The couple, who met at 22 years old, had been expecting their baby for several months and had shared updates on their pregnancy journey. This new addition marks a significant moment for the family, who have been sharing their experiences with their fans on social media.

stories.title: El Euromillones de este martes entrega 250.104,37 euros y deja un nuevo millonario en un municipio popular por sus toros
stories.summary: A Spanish resident in Artana, Castellón, has won 1 million euros in the Euromillones lottery drawing on July 21, 2026, with a valid ticket in the Despacho Receptor no 23.400. The winning combination was 2, 3, 8, 28, and 39, with stars 2 and 11. The next Euromillones drawing will offer a 70 million euro prize for a single winner of the top category.

stories.title: Svolta nella lite tra tennisti e tornei: ecco il passo storico del Roland Garros
stories.summary: The French Open has become the first Grand Slam tournament to offer a direct share of its revenue to players, with a quota to be determined in advance. This move follows months of negotiations between players and tournament organizers, led by Larry Scott, over the distribution of prize money. The Roland Garros decision sets a precedent for other Grand Slam tournaments, including Wimbledon and the Australian Open, which will face pressure to follow suit. The exact details of the agreement are still to be determined.

stories.title: Tiercé. Les pronostics de L'Alsace pour mercredi 22 juillet - Prix Picpus
stories.summary: A 18h30, le Prix Picpus se déroulera à Enghien, avec 15 partants pour la course de trot attelé. Le 9 Montana Kalouma est favori, suivi par le 14 Mourillon et le 4 Meydan Voirons. Le parcours de 2875 mètres, avec corde à gauche, devrait offrir des surprises. Le résultat de cette course sera déterminant pour les instances du trot.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts stopped as star falls ill and Sky Sports cameras pan away
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies, 37, has been forced to withdraw from the World Matchplay Darts tournament after falling ill during his match against Ross Smith at Winter Gardens in Blackpool. Menzies was leading 2-1 when he became unwell, prompting the match to be abandoned. Smith will now receive a bye into the quarter-finals. Menzies has been cleared by medics and is okay, but will not continue playing tonight.

stories.title: Hohe Geldstrafe für den FC Bayern
stories.summary: The German Football Association has fined FC Bayern 260,000 euros for unsportsmanlike behavior by their fans during the DFB-Pokalfinale against VfB Stuttgart on May 23. The fine is also imposed on VfB Stuttgart, which was also fined 260,000 euros. The incident occurred when pyrotechnic devices were set off, causing a three-minute delay in the game. The clubs must also use up to 86,700 euros of the fine for safety measures by December 31.

stories.title: "Secret Story" 2026 : Malo, Léonie, Simon et Maguette nommés... Qui doit rester ? Votez dans notre sondage
stories.summary: Three new contestants have been named to join the "Secret Story" 2026 house, bringing the total to Malo, Léonie, Simon, Maguette, and Kurtis. The latest additions were announced on July 21. Since the competition began, three contestants have left the house, with the most recent being Arwen on July 14. The new contestants will join the existing group, with Kurtis already in the "Grenier" and working with Romy to strategize his return.

stories.title: "¿Quieres que se le recorte la pensión?" La sindicalista Afra Blanco estalla contra una empresaria que pide bajar las cotizaciones
stories.summary: A Spanish businesswoman Tania Tejeira has proposed reducing social security contributions for low-income freelancers, citing a case where an employee receives 3,037 euros and the employer pays 5,780 euros. This would mean the employee only receives 52.5% of the job's cost. Union representative Afra Blanco strongly opposed the idea, warning it could lead to reduced pensions and benefits. The proposal is set to be implemented, with the government already planning to exempt freelancers earning under 85,000 euros from value-added tax.

stories.title: For Monroe Freeling and the offensive line, the first transition begins on Day 1
stories.summary: The Carolina Panthers are starting training camp without their top tackles Ikem Ekwonu and Taylor Moton due to injuries, but the team is confident in their replacements with rookie Monroe Freeling at right tackle and veteran Rasheed Walker at left tackle. The two will start in place of the injured tackles until they return after the regular season. The Panthers drafted Freeling in the first round this year and signed Walker to fill the void. The team's depth and preparation will be put to the test as they navigate the season without their starting tackles.

stories.title: Verdict of accidental death at Kerry teenager's inquest
stories.summary: A 14-year-old boy, Thomas Healy, died in a car crash on July 7th, 2021, in Killarney, Co Kerry, after his car was pursued by a garda van. The inquest returned a verdict of accidental death. Thomas's best friend, who was a passenger, testified that the driver panicked after the van's blue lights appeared. An investigation by the Garda Síochána Ombudsman Commission in 2022 cleared the garda driver of any responsibility.

stories.title: Las razones de la ausencia de Isco Alarcón en el Recre - Betis de pretemporada
stories.summary: Real Betis captain Isco Alarcon is absent from the team's pre-season training due to a plan of preparation and precaution to avoid injuries. He is expected to return in a few days, with the team's next friendly matches against Granada or Lyon. Isco has been repeating that his priority this pre-season is to play without pain. The team is taking a cautious approach to ensure his recovery.

stories.title: MLS schedule Wednesday, July 22: Will Lewandowski debut? Is Messi available?
stories.summary: Major League Soccer returns to full action with 15 matches on Wednesday, July 22, following a six-week break for the 2026 World Cup. Inter Miami hosts Chicago Fire in a highly anticipated match, with Robert Lewandowski potentially making his debut and Lionel Messi's availability still uncertain. The full slate of games includes FC Cincinnati vs Vancouver Whitecaps, Columbus Crew vs New York City FC, and Houston Dynamo vs D.C. United. The outcome of these matches will set the stage for the league's All-Star Game on July 29.

stories.title: “È stata la notte più terrificante della mia vita”: Alberto Rossi perde il suo barboncino Ares, ore di angoscia e poi la svolta
stories.summary: Italian actor Alberto Rossi's dog Ares has been found after a night of frantic searching. The brown barboncino had escaped at Caprera during a bike ride, prompting a desperate search effort. Rossi's friends used a drone and social media to help locate the dog, which was found within a one-kilometer radius of where it was lost. Rossi has expressed gratitude to the community for their support, saying it was one of the most terrifying and beautiful nights of his life.

stories.title: Stevenage vs West Ham LIVE Score Updates in Friendly Match
stories.summary: West Ham United is playing a preseason friendly match against Stevenage at Lamex Stadium on July 22, 2026. The match has sold out to West Ham supporters, with over 1,363 tickets purchased by Season Ticket Holders. The game is being shown live on West Ham TV, with Season Ticket Holders able to watch without paying the usual streaming charge. The match is a key part of West Ham's preparations for the 2026-2027 season.

stories.title: 2 tornadoes confirmed in northern New Jersey as severe weather impacts Tri-State Area
stories.summary: Two tornadoes touched down in northern New Jersey on Tuesday, one in Stanhope, Sussex County, and the other in Parsippany-Troy Hills, Morris County. The National Weather Service will investigate and determine the strength of the tornadoes on Wednesday. No injuries were reported in connection with the tornadoes. Heavy rainfall also affected the Tri-State Area, with totals reaching 4.8 inches in Tarrytown, New York.

stories.title: Al-Hilal agree £60m deal for West Ham's Summerville
stories.summary: West Ham has agreed a £60m deal to sell winger Crysencio Summerville to Saudi club Al-Hilal. The 24-year-old Netherlands international has clearance to undergo a medical after the deal was accepted by the Hammers. This substantial windfall comes as West Ham looks to cut its wage bill and raise funds after being relegated from the Premier League in May. The club is now expected to focus on earning promotion back to the top flight during its 2026-27 campaign.

stories.title: French President Emmanuel Macron asked the Minister for Ecological Transition, Monique Barbut, to remain in office after she announced her resignation, citing pressures on her ecological agenda.
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has asked Minister for Ecological Transition Monique Barbut to remain in office after she announced her resignation due to pressures on her ecological agenda. The two agreed on three key areas: forests, water, and environmental health. Barbut had criticized the government's handling of a controversial agricultural bill. She will continue to serve, with a focus on these critical issues.

stories.title: Morocco urges Italy to explain death of national during arrest in Bologna
stories.summary: A 42-year-old Moroccan national, Abderrahim Fakir, died after being restrained by Italian police in Bologna, prompting an investigation and protests in the city. Italian authorities have opened an inquiry into the incident, with prosecutors examining the circumstances surrounding Fakir's death. Morocco's foreign ministry is demanding a full explanation from Italy, calling for responsibility to be established and legal measures to be taken. The incident is being closely monitored by Moroccan authorities.

stories.title: Hegseth turns to University of North Carolina, Virginia Tech after cutting Ivy League ties
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has selected the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and Virginia Tech to host a revamped fellowship program for senior military officers. The two universities will participate in the 10-month Senior Service College Fellowship program. This move follows Hegseth's earlier decision to bar military officers from attending graduate programs at several elite universities. The program will serve as an alternative to traditional war colleges.

stories.title: French Model Scout Daniel Siad Linked To Epstein Found Dead
stories.summary: French authorities have opened an investigation into the death of Daniel Siad, a modeling scout linked to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, found dead at his home in Colombes, west of Paris. Siad was under investigation over allegations he helped Epstein recruit and traffic young women. He faced a rape allegation and appeared in over 1,000 records connected to Epstein's network. An inquiry is underway to determine the cause of death.

stories.title: Five things we learned from Tottenham 1-0 MK Dons in pre-season as Mateus Fernandes stars
stories.summary: Tottenham Hotspur kicked off pre-season with a 1-0 win over MK Dons in a behind-closed-doors friendly at Hotspur Way. Mateus Fernandes made his debut and scored a goal, with Sandro Tonali also debuting in a midfield two. The match saw 21 players in action, with new signings and returning players testing their fitness. Fernandes' goal will likely be a highlight of the match as Tottenham continues pre-season training.

stories.title: Ab heute bei Netflix: Das erwartet die Zuschauer bei der neuen Serie „Elite Force“
stories.summary: A new series, "GIGN - Elite Force," is now available on Netflix. The eight-part French series premiered on July 22, 2026. The show follows David, a former leader of the French elite unit GIGN, as he takes charge again to hunt down an unknown enemy threatening the team. The series explores David's past and his relationship with his 25-year-old protégé, Max.

stories.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
stories.summary: Wolverhampton Wanderers played a friendly match against Maidenhead United on July 22nd, with the match streamed live on Wolves TV for free. The visitors created numerous chances but were unable to break the deadlock. The match was paused for hydration breaks, allowing the teams to regroup and continue play. The outcome of the match is not specified.

stories.title: Pandur's arrival 'perfect timing' as Rangers add set-piece coach
stories.summary: Rangers have appointed Ivor Pandur as their new set-piece coach, a 26-year-old goalkeeper who joins from Hull City after representing Croatia at the World Cup. Pandur signed a four-year contract and has experience from playing in the Premier League and Italy. He cited glowing recommendations from former Rangers players as a key factor in his decision to join the club. Pandur is now set to work with manager Derek McInnes to improve the team's set-piece performances.

stories.title: Dieser lange indizierte Stephen-King-Horror läuft heute Abend ungekürzt im TV!
stories.summary: Stephen King's horror sequel "Pet Sematary 2" airs uncut tonight on German TV, specifically on ZDFneo at 23:30 UTC. The 1992 film, directed by Mary Lambert and starring Edward Furlong, was a commercial failure, earning only a fraction of its predecessor's box office revenue. It was also banned in Germany for 23 years before being de-indexed and re-released in high definition. Tonight's broadcast marks the first time the film has been shown on free TV in Germany.

stories.title: Vladimir Luxuria rinuncia al Molise Pride, in un video i motivi della scelta
stories.summary: Vladimir Luxuria has withdrawn from the Molise Pride event citing a political and coherent decision. The event, supported by the CGIL Molise, will take place on July 25 in Campobasso. Over 1,000 people are expected to attend, with the CGIL Molise emphasizing the importance of defending democracy and human rights. The event will also focus on issues such as economic inequality and social injustice.

stories.title: Jasmine Crockett 'finds a racism underbelly in every single place': Martha MacCalum
stories.summary: A federal oversight task force holds a hearing on diversity equity and inclusion in law schools, with Representative Jasmine Crockett criticizing the lack of diversity in law school admissions and faculty. The hearing is part of a broader investigation into diversity equity and inclusion policies in law schools. Crockett claims to have found racism in every aspect of law schools. The task force will continue its investigation into the matter.

stories.title: In our opinion: Toyota’s Koji Sato’s Japan commonization strategy is sound, difficult — and familiar
stories.summary: Toyota's Koji Sato is pushing for parts standardization among Japan's automakers to counter China's cost advantages. This strategy, similar to Sergio Marchionne's past vision, aims to reduce production costs. However, U.S. antitrust laws and suppliers' history of price-fixing pose significant challenges to collaboration efforts. Industry experts will closely watch the outcome of this initiative.

stories.title: Bari Sardo, investe una coppia con l’auto e poi uccide a coltellate il ragazzo
stories.summary: A 19-year-old man, Simone Concas, was killed in a stabbing in Bari Sardo, Ogliastra, on an unknown date. He was attacked by the driver of a car who had previously run him over while he was walking with a 25-year-old woman. The woman was also injured and is being treated at a hospital in Cagliari. The attacker is now being sought by law enforcement.

stories.title: 'Nobody ever spoke about her': Australian woman traces family link to Tuam mother and baby home
stories.summary: An Australian woman, Allara Kate Ashton, has made a significant discovery linking her family to the Tuam mother and baby home in Ireland. According to records, an 11-month-old boy died at the home in 1925. Ashton believes the child may be the son of her grandaunt, a connection she hopes DNA evidence will confirm. The revelation sheds new light on the tragic history of the home.

stories.title: Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow after the indictment
stories.summary: Former President José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow after being indicted for influence peddling on May 19. He will speak publicly for the first time since the indictment. Zapatero's interview comes after he was charged with corruption allegations. The interview is set to provide insight into his side of the story.

stories.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
stories.summary: The Italian Senate's Authorisation Committee has rejected prosecutors' request to obtain chat records between Andrea Delmastro and a restaurateur, citing concerns over vagueness and lack of a time frame. The centre-right voted in favour of the proposal, while the centre-left voted against. The Five Star Movement occupied the government benches in protest, holding up placards and shouting slogans. The sitting was suspended, with the occupation lasting over half an hour.

stories.title: JK Rowling offers support to Women’s Rights Network after Féile cancellation
stories.summary: JK Rowling has offered support to Women's Rights Network in Northern Ireland after an event promoting women's sex-based rights was cancelled by Féile an Phobail festival organizers. The event, scheduled for this summer, was targeted by an online campaign described as misogynistic and threatening. Féile an Phobail cited concerns about promoting campaigns against transgender rights, but the Women's Rights Network claims its event was clearly about women's rights. The network plans to continue its work despite the cancellation.

stories.title: Inside the Gen Z Film Publicity Stunt That Had Manosphere Poster Boy Kyle University Stationed Outside L.A., London Studios (Exclusive)
stories.summary: A viral social media campaign, "Kyle University," has garnered over 60 million views and 30,000 followers, featuring a character promoting manosphere ideals. However, it's been revealed as a satirical stunt to promote the film "Impressionable," directed by 22-year-old British director Noah Caplan. The campaign, which included costumed "manosphere recruiters" at major studios, has successfully drawn attention to the film. The film's premiere is now expected to generate significant interest.

stories.title: Anicia Berton, militante martiniquaise, est décédée
stories.summary: Anicia Berton, a prominent Martinican activist, has died. She was 52 years old and led the association "Nasyon Matinik". Berton fought for various causes including pesticide exposure, reparations for slavery, and social and environmental justice. Her death has sparked tributes and condolences from the community. Her legacy is expected to continue inspiring social change in Martinique.

stories.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
stories.summary: Fifteen people were injured in a tram collision on the Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, with one person seriously hurt. Five were taken to the hospital, while the others were treated on the scene. The incident occurred around 11:10 am. The Rotterdam mayor has described the accident as "a terrible disaster".

stories.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
stories.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m if certain conditions are met. The 22-year-old has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. This deal follows Chelsea's signing of Morgan Rogers from Villa for a record £117m fee. Garnacho's departure from Stamford Bridge comes after just nine months, following his £40m transfer from Manchester United.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant in response to ongoing attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The potential targets include those located near the capital Tehran. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran and follows reports of air defences being activated in Tehran. The US has not specified when it will carry out the threatened strikes.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA is set to unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility will be showcased in a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will be available for a brief media availability. The event is open to US citizens and lawful permanent residents, with RSVPs due by July 29.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: The UK's Conservative party has asked HMRC to investigate a £5 million gift to Nigel Farage, questioning whether it should be taxed as compensation for his failed bid to become an MP. The gift was given by crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne, who claims it was unconditional and for personal reasons. Farage has previously stated the money was for personal security costs and a Brexit campaign reward. HMRC will now determine if the gift should be subject to tax.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A 15-year-old Florida plaintiff has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, claiming the company's social media platforms caused his addiction and harm. The case was set to go before a Los Angeles jury next week. Meta denies the claims, stating they never held up in court. The teenager had previously settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube.

stories.title: | Comunicado Oficial | Información LVII Trofeo Colombino
stories.summary: A large crowd is expected at the RC Recreativo de Huelva's Trofeo Colombino match on Wednesday, with fans advised to arrive early at the Nuevo Colombino stadium due to high attendance. Parking will be limited, with only a designated area near Gol Norte accessible from Avenida del Decano. The event is part of the Fiestas Colombinas celebrations. Traffic congestion is anticipated, potentially causing delays at the stadium's entrances.

stories.title: EastEnders confirms Joel's fate amid death fears in new advanced spoilers
stories.summary: EastEnders confirmed Joel Marshall's fate in advanced spoilers, revealing he survives a potentially fatal attack in the Square. The 22 July episode saw Joel locked to a railing and doused in alcohol, but he was later found face-down after being hit on the head. His father Ross remains determined to help him. Joel's survival will be a key plot point in the upcoming storyline.

stories.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar has resigned to join Andy Burnham's government, where he will become a minister at the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. He will also be made a peer, a decision that has been criticized by Scottish Green party co-leader Gillian Mackay as "hypocrisy" given his previous stance against the House of Lords. The move has sparked calls for Sarwar to donate his new salary to charity. The consequences of this decision remain to be seen.

stories.title: Wetter morgen am Donnerstag: Einzelne Schauer – regional bis zu 27 Grad
stories.summary: Heavy rain and thunderstorms are expected to hit eastern Germany on Thursday, with temperatures ranging from 16 to 27 degrees Celsius in different regions. The rain is expected to affect areas from the East Sea to the Erzgebirge, with occasional light rain in parts of Oberfranken. In contrast, the southwest and Schleswig-Holstein are expected to remain mostly sunny. Temperatures are expected to rise in the north, reaching up to 27 degrees in the Breisgau region.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic in a 3-2 win at Motherwell, kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes is contesting the ban, saying his comments were directed at the decision, not officials. He will serve the ban, but will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership season.

stories.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for describing a late penalty awarded to Celtic as "disgusting" in May. The penalty, given for handball, was converted by Kelechi Iheanacho in Celtic's 3-2 win over Motherwell on May 13. McInnes will serve the ban, with one match suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership season.

stories.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
stories.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement outlines €1.5 billion for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set for October. This will be accompanied by a €7 billion increase in spending next year, bringing total expenditure to €125 billion in 2027, a 5.9% rise from 2026. The tax package and spending increases total €8.5 billion. The government will now review its reliance on corporation tax.

stories.title: Que regarder à la TV ce soir ?
stories.summary: Tonight, a new game show premieres on M6 at 21:10, challenging contestants to navigate everyday situations and consumer reflexes. The show promises a light-hearted and interactive evening with a mix of common sense and everyday pitfalls. Viewers can also tune in to France 5 for a heartwarming reunion show, or to France 2 for a family drama that takes an unexpected turn. The evening's lineup offers a range of options for viewers.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, has been found dead at his home in Colombes, a north-west suburb of Paris. An investigation into his death was opened on Monday evening, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad's name appeared thousands of times in Epstein files, and he had been the subject of a Paris prosecutor's office investigation launched in February. The investigation into Siad's death is ongoing.

stories.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham is facing controversy just three days into office, accused of performing U-turns on key policies including raising the income tax threshold and freezing private sector rents. The prime minister has clarified that raising the income tax threshold remains an option, despite earlier reports suggesting it was ruled out. Burnham's unscripted comments have sparked headaches for his team, with critics questioning his approach to policy-making. His office will now need to navigate the fallout from these minor controversies.

stories.title: Machtwechsel im Osten?: Alle Daten zur Landtagswahl in Sachsen-Anhalt
stories.summary: In the upcoming September 1st election, approximately 1.7 million eligible voters in Saxony-Anhalt will determine the power dynamics in the state parliament. According to recent polls, the Alternative for Germany party is leading with up to 41 percent, potentially making it the strongest force in the state. This could result in the party's leader, Ulrich Siegmund, becoming the new Minister-President. The outcome of the election will be closely watched as it may signal a shift in power in the region.

stories.title: Espagne-Algérie : ce que cache vraiment la réconciliation
stories.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez met with Algerian President Abdelmadjid Tebboune in Algiers on July 20, 2026, marking the end of four years of diplomatic crisis between the two countries. The meeting follows Sánchez's visit to New York, where he attended the World Cup. The reconciliation comes without a change in Spain's position on the Western Sahara issue. The two leaders' meeting is seen as a significant step towards normalizing relations.

stories.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
stories.summary: Hyundai Motor Company is disputing reports that its humanoid robot plan is part of current negotiations with striking South Korean autoworkers. The company claims the union's demands focus on compensation-related issues, not robot deployment. Hyundai's plan to deploy humanoid robots in the US starting in 2028 is separate from labor talks. The company remains committed to constructive engagement with the union to reach a long-term agreement.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has reversed his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of authority to do so. Mamdani now calls on US officials to act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for alleged war crimes. The US is not a member of the ICC, which could lead to Netanyahu's arrest if he enters an ICC member state. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump vowed to prevent Netanyahu's arrest while in the US.

stories.title: Oficial: la Roja jugará amistoso ante una selección mundialista
stories.summary: La selección chilena, conocida como La Roja, jugará un amistoso contra Canadá el 26 de septiembre en Toronto. Canadá fue segundo en su grupo en la última Copa Mundial y eliminó a Sudáfrica en la ronda de 32. Este partido será el primer encuentro de La Roja después de los partidos ante Portugal y Congo. La Roja buscará una victoria en su regreso a la actividad.

stories.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
stories.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

stories.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
stories.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

stories.title: Gil Vicente-FC Porto: canta o galo para a nova temporada
stories.summary: FC Porto defeated Gil Vicente 3-0 in a preseason match at the Estádio Cidade de Barcelos on Wednesday. The game was part of the Galos' presentation to their supporters. This is the second win for FC Porto in their preseason, following a 2-1 victory over Birmingham. The team will face another match on Saturday.

stories.title: City celebrates Ozzy Day on anniversary of death
stories.summary: Birmingham is marking the one-year anniversary of Ozzy Osbourne's death with a day of music and celebration, affectionately called "Ozzy Day". Fans have gathered in the city, wearing tribute T-shirts and visiting sites connected to the heavy metal icon. Thousands have attended the event, including some who traveled internationally to pay their respects. The city's celebration will continue with music and tributes to Osbourne's legacy.

stories.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads tributes to Kaylee Hottle
stories.summary: US actress Kaylee Hottle, 18, died in a single-vehicle car crash in Frederick, Maryland on Tuesday morning. Hottle, who was deaf, appeared in the 2021 film Godzilla vs Kong and its 2024 sequel. Tributes poured in from co-stars including Millie Bobby Brown, Rebecca Hall, and Eiza González, who remembered her kindness and talent. The film industry is mourning the loss of the young actress.

stories.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report targets ultra-processed foods, citing sugar and refined carbohydrates as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee recommends a new approach to food policy, shifting focus from "high in fat, salt or sugar" to specific nutrient targets. This change aims to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for healthier products.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
stories.summary: David J. Ascione, a 62-year-old lifelong resident of Toms River, New Jersey, passed away on July 19, 2026. He was a member of the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company and worked at his family's business, Ascione Florist and Greenhouse. Ascione will be remembered by his family and friends. A private funeral will be held for immediate family only.
stories.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 out of 183 tested towers have confirmed positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to test the remaining towers, with results expected in the coming week. The number of new cases has remained steady, with no new symptoms reported in over 10 days.

offbeat.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
offbeat.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal with Chelsea for 22-year-old winger Alejandro Garnacho, with an obligation to buy. The deal is for one year, with the option to make it permanent next summer if certain conditions are met. Chelsea will receive £43m if the deal becomes permanent. Garnacho has already completed a medical and is set to sign a four-year contract at Villa Park.

offbeat.title: French modeling scout with links to Epstein found dead
offbeat.summary: French modeling scout Daniel Siad, 69, with ties to convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been found dead at his home outside Paris. Siad was under investigation in France over allegations of aiding Epstein to traffic and abuse women, and was accused of rape and human trafficking by at least five women. An autopsy will be carried out to determine the cause of death, which authorities have not yet confirmed. The investigation into Siad's alleged crimes will continue despite his death.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

offbeat.title: Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 MK Dons: Top 10 talking points
offbeat.summary: Tottenham Hotspur began their preseason campaign with a 1-0 win over MK Dons in a behind-closed-doors friendly at their training ground. Mateus Fernandes scored the lone goal with a stunning strike, marking his debut along with new signings Sandro Tonali and Martin Dubravka. The match saw a mix of youth and experience, with several players missing due to the World Cup. Tottenham will look to build on this momentum as they continue their preseason training.

offbeat.title: Rosalía breaks her silence after sparking outrage among Argentine fans: “My bad, sorry."
offbeat.summary: Spanish singer Rosalía has apologized for sparking outrage among Argentine fans after she reposted a social media video that many viewed as a jab at their national team. The incident occurred during the World Cup. Rosalía deleted the post and issued a brief apology, stating she had shared the video without reading its caption or understanding the message. Her apology comes as criticism spread online.

offbeat.title: Aston Villa Launch Loan Bid For £42.5 Outcast: Is He Needed For Unai Emery?
offbeat.summary: Aston Villa has launched a loan bid for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho, with a £42.5 million price tag attached to a permanent transfer. The 22-year-old Argentine international will join Villa Park on loan with a buy clause that will become active once certain conditions are met. Garnacho's departure from Chelsea marks the end of a frustrating first campaign in west London, where he netted once and provided four assists in 24 Premier League games. Unai Emery's interest in the winger is not new, and a medical exam has been planned for the incoming player.

offbeat.title: Unai Emery Convinces Aston Villa to Complete Move for Alejandro Garnacho: Reports
offbeat.summary: Aston Villa have agreed to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea on loan with a conditional buy clause. The 22-year-old Argentina winger will undergo a medical before finalizing the move. Villa’s interest follows Unai Emery’s pursuit, with Chelsea set to receive around £42.5 million if conditions are met. The deal comes as Garnacho’s first season at Stamford Bridge fell short, producing just one goal and four assists in 24 Premier League appearances.

offbeat.title: Romano: Summerville accepts Al Hilal’s offer
offbeat.summary: Crysencio Summerville has accepted Al Hilal's offer to join the Saudi Pro League club, with a base fee of £55 million and potential add-ons of £10 million. The 24-year-old Dutch winger is on his way to Saudi to undergo a medical examination. This move represents a significant profit for West Ham United, who signed Summerville in 2024. Al Hilal's ambitious bid has secured the signing, with Summerville set to strengthen the Saudi club's squad.

offbeat.title: Peter Lim visita Valencia para ver en persona a Inma Ibáñez y supervisar el progreso del Nou Mestalla
offbeat.summary: Valencia CF's owner Peter Lim has returned to Valencia, Spain, after attending the World Cup final in the US, where Spain won its second star. Lim visited the Nou Mestalla stadium, the future home of Valencia CF, accompanied by top club officials. The stadium is in a new phase of construction after the installation of a compression ring and 50 steel pillars. Lim also met with Inma Ibáñez, the club's director of the foundation, and discussed the club's summer market situation.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 LIVE RESULTS: Play SUSPENDED during Menzies' match
offbeat.summary: Play at the World Matchplay in Blackpool was suspended due to Cameron Menzies' apparent illness, forcing his withdrawal from the tournament. Menzies was leading Ross Smith 2-1 when he slumped over and was helped off the stage. He has been cleared by medics but will not return to continue his match. Smith has progressed to the quarter-finals as a result. The night's second match, Gian van Veen vs Wessel Nijman, has been brought forward.

offbeat.title: El Supremo admite el recurso para parar las altas en el censo por la Ley de Nietos y pide audiencia a la JEC
offbeat.summary: The Spanish Supreme Court has admitted a lawsuit to halt the implementation of the "Law of Grandchildren" in the foreign voter census, following a complaint by the party Iustitia Europa. The law allows grandchildren of Spanish citizens to vote, even if they have never lived in Spain. The court has given the Electoral Central Board 10 days to respond to the suspension request. The case is set to be heard in September, with potential implications for the 2.5 million people who have applied for Spanish citizenship.

offbeat.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
offbeat.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas have welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, on July 21. The couple, who met at 22 years old, had been expecting their baby for several months and had shared updates on their pregnancy journey. This new addition marks a significant moment for the family, who have been sharing their experiences with their fans on social media.

offbeat.title: El Euromillones de este martes entrega 250.104,37 euros y deja un nuevo millonario en un municipio popular por sus toros
offbeat.summary: A Spanish resident in Artana, Castellón, has won 1 million euros in the Euromillones lottery drawing on July 21, 2026, with a valid ticket in the Despacho Receptor no 23.400. The winning combination was 2, 3, 8, 28, and 39, with stars 2 and 11. The next Euromillones drawing will offer a 70 million euro prize for a single winner of the top category.

offbeat.title: Svolta nella lite tra tennisti e tornei: ecco il passo storico del Roland Garros
offbeat.summary: The French Open has become the first Grand Slam tournament to offer a direct share of its revenue to players, with a quota to be determined in advance. This move follows months of negotiations between players and tournament organizers, led by Larry Scott, over the distribution of prize money. The Roland Garros decision sets a precedent for other Grand Slam tournaments, including Wimbledon and the Australian Open, which will face pressure to follow suit. The exact details of the agreement are still to be determined.

offbeat.title: Tiercé. Les pronostics de L'Alsace pour mercredi 22 juillet - Prix Picpus
offbeat.summary: A 18h30, le Prix Picpus se déroulera à Enghien, avec 15 partants pour la course de trot attelé. Le 9 Montana Kalouma est favori, suivi par le 14 Mourillon et le 4 Meydan Voirons. Le parcours de 2875 mètres, avec corde à gauche, devrait offrir des surprises. Le résultat de cette course sera déterminant pour les instances du trot.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts stopped as star falls ill and Sky Sports cameras pan away
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies, 37, has been forced to withdraw from the World Matchplay Darts tournament after falling ill during his match against Ross Smith at Winter Gardens in Blackpool. Menzies was leading 2-1 when he became unwell, prompting the match to be abandoned. Smith will now receive a bye into the quarter-finals. Menzies has been cleared by medics and is okay, but will not continue playing tonight.

offbeat.title: Hohe Geldstrafe für den FC Bayern
offbeat.summary: The German Football Association has fined FC Bayern 260,000 euros for unsportsmanlike behavior by their fans during the DFB-Pokalfinale against VfB Stuttgart on May 23. The fine is also imposed on VfB Stuttgart, which was also fined 260,000 euros. The incident occurred when pyrotechnic devices were set off, causing a three-minute delay in the game. The clubs must also use up to 86,700 euros of the fine for safety measures by December 31.

offbeat.title: "Secret Story" 2026 : Malo, Léonie, Simon et Maguette nommés... Qui doit rester ? Votez dans notre sondage
offbeat.summary: Three new contestants have been named to join the "Secret Story" 2026 house, bringing the total to Malo, Léonie, Simon, Maguette, and Kurtis. The latest additions were announced on July 21. Since the competition began, three contestants have left the house, with the most recent being Arwen on July 14. The new contestants will join the existing group, with Kurtis already in the "Grenier" and working with Romy to strategize his return.

offbeat.title: "¿Quieres que se le recorte la pensión?" La sindicalista Afra Blanco estalla contra una empresaria que pide bajar las cotizaciones
offbeat.summary: A Spanish businesswoman Tania Tejeira has proposed reducing social security contributions for low-income freelancers, citing a case where an employee receives 3,037 euros and the employer pays 5,780 euros. This would mean the employee only receives 52.5% of the job's cost. Union representative Afra Blanco strongly opposed the idea, warning it could lead to reduced pensions and benefits. The proposal is set to be implemented, with the government already planning to exempt freelancers earning under 85,000 euros from value-added tax.

offbeat.title: For Monroe Freeling and the offensive line, the first transition begins on Day 1
offbeat.summary: The Carolina Panthers are starting training camp without their top tackles Ikem Ekwonu and Taylor Moton due to injuries, but the team is confident in their replacements with rookie Monroe Freeling at right tackle and veteran Rasheed Walker at left tackle. The two will start in place of the injured tackles until they return after the regular season. The Panthers drafted Freeling in the first round this year and signed Walker to fill the void. The team's depth and preparation will be put to the test as they navigate the season without their starting tackles.

offbeat.title: Verdict of accidental death at Kerry teenager's inquest
offbeat.summary: A 14-year-old boy, Thomas Healy, died in a car crash on July 7th, 2021, in Killarney, Co Kerry, after his car was pursued by a garda van. The inquest returned a verdict of accidental death. Thomas's best friend, who was a passenger, testified that the driver panicked after the van's blue lights appeared. An investigation by the Garda Síochána Ombudsman Commission in 2022 cleared the garda driver of any responsibility.

offbeat.title: Las razones de la ausencia de Isco Alarcón en el Recre - Betis de pretemporada
offbeat.summary: Real Betis captain Isco Alarcon is absent from the team's pre-season training due to a plan of preparation and precaution to avoid injuries. He is expected to return in a few days, with the team's next friendly matches against Granada or Lyon. Isco has been repeating that his priority this pre-season is to play without pain. The team is taking a cautious approach to ensure his recovery.

offbeat.title: MLS schedule Wednesday, July 22: Will Lewandowski debut? Is Messi available?
offbeat.summary: Major League Soccer returns to full action with 15 matches on Wednesday, July 22, following a six-week break for the 2026 World Cup. Inter Miami hosts Chicago Fire in a highly anticipated match, with Robert Lewandowski potentially making his debut and Lionel Messi's availability still uncertain. The full slate of games includes FC Cincinnati vs Vancouver Whitecaps, Columbus Crew vs New York City FC, and Houston Dynamo vs D.C. United. The outcome of these matches will set the stage for the league's All-Star Game on July 29.

offbeat.title: “È stata la notte più terrificante della mia vita”: Alberto Rossi perde il suo barboncino Ares, ore di angoscia e poi la svolta
offbeat.summary: Italian actor Alberto Rossi's dog Ares has been found after a night of frantic searching. The brown barboncino had escaped at Caprera during a bike ride, prompting a desperate search effort. Rossi's friends used a drone and social media to help locate the dog, which was found within a one-kilometer radius of where it was lost. Rossi has expressed gratitude to the community for their support, saying it was one of the most terrifying and beautiful nights of his life.

offbeat.title: Stevenage vs West Ham LIVE Score Updates in Friendly Match
offbeat.summary: West Ham United is playing a preseason friendly match against Stevenage at Lamex Stadium on July 22, 2026. The match has sold out to West Ham supporters, with over 1,363 tickets purchased by Season Ticket Holders. The game is being shown live on West Ham TV, with Season Ticket Holders able to watch without paying the usual streaming charge. The match is a key part of West Ham's preparations for the 2026-2027 season.

offbeat.title: 2 tornadoes confirmed in northern New Jersey as severe weather impacts Tri-State Area
offbeat.summary: Two tornadoes touched down in northern New Jersey on Tuesday, one in Stanhope, Sussex County, and the other in Parsippany-Troy Hills, Morris County. The National Weather Service will investigate and determine the strength of the tornadoes on Wednesday. No injuries were reported in connection with the tornadoes. Heavy rainfall also affected the Tri-State Area, with totals reaching 4.8 inches in Tarrytown, New York.

offbeat.title: Al-Hilal agree £60m deal for West Ham's Summerville
offbeat.summary: West Ham has agreed a £60m deal to sell winger Crysencio Summerville to Saudi club Al-Hilal. The 24-year-old Netherlands international has clearance to undergo a medical after the deal was accepted by the Hammers. This substantial windfall comes as West Ham looks to cut its wage bill and raise funds after being relegated from the Premier League in May. The club is now expected to focus on earning promotion back to the top flight during its 2026-27 campaign.

offbeat.title: French President Emmanuel Macron asked the Minister for Ecological Transition, Monique Barbut, to remain in office after she announced her resignation, citing pressures on her ecological agenda.
offbeat.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has asked Minister for Ecological Transition Monique Barbut to remain in office after she announced her resignation due to pressures on her ecological agenda. The two agreed on three key areas: forests, water, and environmental health. Barbut had criticized the government's handling of a controversial agricultural bill. She will continue to serve, with a focus on these critical issues.

offbeat.title: Morocco urges Italy to explain death of national during arrest in Bologna
offbeat.summary: A 42-year-old Moroccan national, Abderrahim Fakir, died after being restrained by Italian police in Bologna, prompting an investigation and protests in the city. Italian authorities have opened an inquiry into the incident, with prosecutors examining the circumstances surrounding Fakir's death. Morocco's foreign ministry is demanding a full explanation from Italy, calling for responsibility to be established and legal measures to be taken. The incident is being closely monitored by Moroccan authorities.

offbeat.title: Hegseth turns to University of North Carolina, Virginia Tech after cutting Ivy League ties
offbeat.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has selected the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and Virginia Tech to host a revamped fellowship program for senior military officers. The two universities will participate in the 10-month Senior Service College Fellowship program. This move follows Hegseth's earlier decision to bar military officers from attending graduate programs at several elite universities. The program will serve as an alternative to traditional war colleges.

offbeat.title: French Model Scout Daniel Siad Linked To Epstein Found Dead
offbeat.summary: French authorities have opened an investigation into the death of Daniel Siad, a modeling scout linked to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, found dead at his home in Colombes, west of Paris. Siad was under investigation over allegations he helped Epstein recruit and traffic young women. He faced a rape allegation and appeared in over 1,000 records connected to Epstein's network. An inquiry is underway to determine the cause of death.

offbeat.title: Five things we learned from Tottenham 1-0 MK Dons in pre-season as Mateus Fernandes stars
offbeat.summary: Tottenham Hotspur kicked off pre-season with a 1-0 win over MK Dons in a behind-closed-doors friendly at Hotspur Way. Mateus Fernandes made his debut and scored a goal, with Sandro Tonali also debuting in a midfield two. The match saw 21 players in action, with new signings and returning players testing their fitness. Fernandes' goal will likely be a highlight of the match as Tottenham continues pre-season training.

offbeat.title: Ab heute bei Netflix: Das erwartet die Zuschauer bei der neuen Serie „Elite Force“
offbeat.summary: A new series, "GIGN - Elite Force," is now available on Netflix. The eight-part French series premiered on July 22, 2026. The show follows David, a former leader of the French elite unit GIGN, as he takes charge again to hunt down an unknown enemy threatening the team. The series explores David's past and his relationship with his 25-year-old protégé, Max.

offbeat.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
offbeat.summary: Wolverhampton Wanderers played a friendly match against Maidenhead United on July 22nd, with the match streamed live on Wolves TV for free. The visitors created numerous chances but were unable to break the deadlock. The match was paused for hydration breaks, allowing the teams to regroup and continue play. The outcome of the match is not specified.

offbeat.title: Pandur's arrival 'perfect timing' as Rangers add set-piece coach
offbeat.summary: Rangers have appointed Ivor Pandur as their new set-piece coach, a 26-year-old goalkeeper who joins from Hull City after representing Croatia at the World Cup. Pandur signed a four-year contract and has experience from playing in the Premier League and Italy. He cited glowing recommendations from former Rangers players as a key factor in his decision to join the club. Pandur is now set to work with manager Derek McInnes to improve the team's set-piece performances.

offbeat.title: Dieser lange indizierte Stephen-King-Horror läuft heute Abend ungekürzt im TV!
offbeat.summary: Stephen King's horror sequel "Pet Sematary 2" airs uncut tonight on German TV, specifically on ZDFneo at 23:30 UTC. The 1992 film, directed by Mary Lambert and starring Edward Furlong, was a commercial failure, earning only a fraction of its predecessor's box office revenue. It was also banned in Germany for 23 years before being de-indexed and re-released in high definition. Tonight's broadcast marks the first time the film has been shown on free TV in Germany.

offbeat.title: Vladimir Luxuria rinuncia al Molise Pride, in un video i motivi della scelta
offbeat.summary: Vladimir Luxuria has withdrawn from the Molise Pride event citing a political and coherent decision. The event, supported by the CGIL Molise, will take place on July 25 in Campobasso. Over 1,000 people are expected to attend, with the CGIL Molise emphasizing the importance of defending democracy and human rights. The event will also focus on issues such as economic inequality and social injustice.

offbeat.title: Jasmine Crockett 'finds a racism underbelly in every single place': Martha MacCalum
offbeat.summary: A federal oversight task force holds a hearing on diversity equity and inclusion in law schools, with Representative Jasmine Crockett criticizing the lack of diversity in law school admissions and faculty. The hearing is part of a broader investigation into diversity equity and inclusion policies in law schools. Crockett claims to have found racism in every aspect of law schools. The task force will continue its investigation into the matter.

offbeat.title: In our opinion: Toyota’s Koji Sato’s Japan commonization strategy is sound, difficult — and familiar
offbeat.summary: Toyota's Koji Sato is pushing for parts standardization among Japan's automakers to counter China's cost advantages. This strategy, similar to Sergio Marchionne's past vision, aims to reduce production costs. However, U.S. antitrust laws and suppliers' history of price-fixing pose significant challenges to collaboration efforts. Industry experts will closely watch the outcome of this initiative.

offbeat.title: Bari Sardo, investe una coppia con l’auto e poi uccide a coltellate il ragazzo
offbeat.summary: A 19-year-old man, Simone Concas, was killed in a stabbing in Bari Sardo, Ogliastra, on an unknown date. He was attacked by the driver of a car who had previously run him over while he was walking with a 25-year-old woman. The woman was also injured and is being treated at a hospital in Cagliari. The attacker is now being sought by law enforcement.

offbeat.title: 'Nobody ever spoke about her': Australian woman traces family link to Tuam mother and baby home
offbeat.summary: An Australian woman, Allara Kate Ashton, has made a significant discovery linking her family to the Tuam mother and baby home in Ireland. According to records, an 11-month-old boy died at the home in 1925. Ashton believes the child may be the son of her grandaunt, a connection she hopes DNA evidence will confirm. The revelation sheds new light on the tragic history of the home.

offbeat.title: Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow after the indictment
offbeat.summary: Former President José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow after being indicted for influence peddling on May 19. He will speak publicly for the first time since the indictment. Zapatero's interview comes after he was charged with corruption allegations. The interview is set to provide insight into his side of the story.

offbeat.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
offbeat.summary: The Italian Senate's Authorisation Committee has rejected prosecutors' request to obtain chat records between Andrea Delmastro and a restaurateur, citing concerns over vagueness and lack of a time frame. The centre-right voted in favour of the proposal, while the centre-left voted against. The Five Star Movement occupied the government benches in protest, holding up placards and shouting slogans. The sitting was suspended, with the occupation lasting over half an hour.

offbeat.title: JK Rowling offers support to Women’s Rights Network after Féile cancellation
offbeat.summary: JK Rowling has offered support to Women's Rights Network in Northern Ireland after an event promoting women's sex-based rights was cancelled by Féile an Phobail festival organizers. The event, scheduled for this summer, was targeted by an online campaign described as misogynistic and threatening. Féile an Phobail cited concerns about promoting campaigns against transgender rights, but the Women's Rights Network claims its event was clearly about women's rights. The network plans to continue its work despite the cancellation.

offbeat.title: Anicia Berton, militante martiniquaise, est décédée
offbeat.summary: Anicia Berton, a prominent Martinican activist, has died. She was 52 years old and led the association "Nasyon Matinik". Berton fought for various causes including pesticide exposure, reparations for slavery, and social and environmental justice. Her death has sparked tributes and condolences from the community. Her legacy is expected to continue inspiring social change in Martinique.

offbeat.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
offbeat.summary: Fifteen people were injured in a tram collision on the Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, with one person seriously hurt. Five were taken to the hospital, while the others were treated on the scene. The incident occurred around 11:10 am. The Rotterdam mayor has described the accident as "a terrible disaster".

offbeat.title: | Comunicado Oficial | Información LVII Trofeo Colombino
offbeat.summary: A large crowd is expected at the RC Recreativo de Huelva's Trofeo Colombino match on Wednesday, with fans advised to arrive early at the Nuevo Colombino stadium due to high attendance. Parking will be limited, with only a designated area near Gol Norte accessible from Avenida del Decano. The event is part of the Fiestas Colombinas celebrations. Traffic congestion is anticipated, potentially causing delays at the stadium's entrances.

offbeat.title: EastEnders confirms Joel's fate amid death fears in new advanced spoilers
offbeat.summary: EastEnders confirmed Joel Marshall's fate in advanced spoilers, revealing he survives a potentially fatal attack in the Square. The 22 July episode saw Joel locked to a railing and doused in alcohol, but he was later found face-down after being hit on the head. His father Ross remains determined to help him. Joel's survival will be a key plot point in the upcoming storyline.

offbeat.title: Wetter morgen am Donnerstag: Einzelne Schauer – regional bis zu 27 Grad
offbeat.summary: Heavy rain and thunderstorms are expected to hit eastern Germany on Thursday, with temperatures ranging from 16 to 27 degrees Celsius in different regions. The rain is expected to affect areas from the East Sea to the Erzgebirge, with occasional light rain in parts of Oberfranken. In contrast, the southwest and Schleswig-Holstein are expected to remain mostly sunny. Temperatures are expected to rise in the north, reaching up to 27 degrees in the Breisgau region.

offbeat.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
offbeat.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement outlines €1.5 billion for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set for October. This will be accompanied by a €7 billion increase in spending next year, bringing total expenditure to €125 billion in 2027, a 5.9% rise from 2026. The tax package and spending increases total €8.5 billion. The government will now review its reliance on corporation tax.

offbeat.title: Que regarder à la TV ce soir ?
offbeat.summary: Tonight, a new game show premieres on M6 at 21:10, challenging contestants to navigate everyday situations and consumer reflexes. The show promises a light-hearted and interactive evening with a mix of common sense and everyday pitfalls. Viewers can also tune in to France 5 for a heartwarming reunion show, or to France 2 for a family drama that takes an unexpected turn. The evening's lineup offers a range of options for viewers.

offbeat.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
offbeat.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, has been found dead at his home in Colombes, a north-west suburb of Paris. An investigation into his death was opened on Monday evening, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad's name appeared thousands of times in Epstein files, and he had been the subject of a Paris prosecutor's office investigation launched in February. The investigation into Siad's death is ongoing.

offbeat.title: Machtwechsel im Osten?: Alle Daten zur Landtagswahl in Sachsen-Anhalt
offbeat.summary: In the upcoming September 1st election, approximately 1.7 million eligible voters in Saxony-Anhalt will determine the power dynamics in the state parliament. According to recent polls, the Alternative for Germany party is leading with up to 41 percent, potentially making it the strongest force in the state. This could result in the party's leader, Ulrich Siegmund, becoming the new Minister-President. The outcome of the election will be closely watched as it may signal a shift in power in the region.

offbeat.title: Espagne-Algérie : ce que cache vraiment la réconciliation
offbeat.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez met with Algerian President Abdelmadjid Tebboune in Algiers on July 20, 2026, marking the end of four years of diplomatic crisis between the two countries. The meeting follows Sánchez's visit to New York, where he attended the World Cup. The reconciliation comes without a change in Spain's position on the Western Sahara issue. The two leaders' meeting is seen as a significant step towards normalizing relations.

offbeat.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
offbeat.summary: Hyundai Motor Company is disputing reports that its humanoid robot plan is part of current negotiations with striking South Korean autoworkers. The company claims the union's demands focus on compensation-related issues, not robot deployment. Hyundai's plan to deploy humanoid robots in the US starting in 2028 is separate from labor talks. The company remains committed to constructive engagement with the union to reach a long-term agreement.

offbeat.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
offbeat.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has reversed his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of authority to do so. Mamdani now calls on US officials to act on the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for alleged war crimes. The US is not a member of the ICC, which could lead to Netanyahu's arrest if he enters an ICC member state. Mamdani's reversal comes after President Donald Trump vowed to prevent Netanyahu's arrest while in the US.

offbeat.title: Oficial: la Roja jugará amistoso ante una selección mundialista
offbeat.summary: La selección chilena, conocida como La Roja, jugará un amistoso contra Canadá el 26 de septiembre en Toronto. Canadá fue segundo en su grupo en la última Copa Mundial y eliminó a Sudáfrica en la ronda de 32. Este partido será el primer encuentro de La Roja después de los partidos ante Portugal y Congo. La Roja buscará una victoria en su regreso a la actividad.

offbeat.title: Gil Vicente-FC Porto: canta o galo para a nova temporada
offbeat.summary: FC Porto defeated Gil Vicente 3-0 in a preseason match at the Estádio Cidade de Barcelos on Wednesday. The game was part of the Galos' presentation to their supporters. This is the second win for FC Porto in their preseason, following a 2-1 victory over Birmingham. The team will face another match on Saturday.

offbeat.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
offbeat.summary: David J. Ascione, a 62-year-old lifelong resident of Toms River, New Jersey, passed away on July 19, 2026. He was a member of the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company and worked at his family's business, Ascione Florist and Greenhouse. Ascione will be remembered by his family and friends. A private funeral will be held for immediate family only.
offbeat.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

entertainment.title: Inside the Gen Z Film Publicity Stunt That Had Manosphere Poster Boy Kyle University Stationed Outside L.A., London Studios (Exclusive)
entertainment.summary: A viral social media campaign, "Kyle University," has garnered over 60 million views and 30,000 followers, featuring a character promoting manosphere ideals. However, it's been revealed as a satirical stunt to promote the film "Impressionable," directed by 22-year-old British director Noah Caplan. The campaign, which included costumed "manosphere recruiters" at major studios, has successfully drawn attention to the film. The film's premiere is now expected to generate significant interest.

entertainment.title: City celebrates Ozzy Day on anniversary of death
entertainment.summary: Birmingham is marking the one-year anniversary of Ozzy Osbourne's death with a day of music and celebration, affectionately called "Ozzy Day". Fans have gathered in the city, wearing tribute T-shirts and visiting sites connected to the heavy metal icon. Thousands have attended the event, including some who traveled internationally to pay their respects. The city's celebration will continue with music and tributes to Osbourne's legacy.

entertainment.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads tributes to Kaylee Hottle
entertainment.summary: US actress Kaylee Hottle, 18, died in a single-vehicle car crash in Frederick, Maryland on Tuesday morning. Hottle, who was deaf, appeared in the 2021 film Godzilla vs Kong and its 2024 sequel. Tributes poured in from co-stars including Millie Bobby Brown, Rebecca Hall, and Eiza González, who remembered her kindness and talent. The film industry is mourning the loss of the young actress.

sports.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
sports.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m if certain conditions are met. The 22-year-old has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. This deal follows Chelsea's signing of Morgan Rogers from Villa for a record £117m fee. Garnacho's departure from Stamford Bridge comes after just nine months, following his £40m transfer from Manchester United.

sports.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic in a 3-2 win at Motherwell, kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes is contesting the ban, saying his comments were directed at the decision, not officials. He will serve the ban, but will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership season.

sports.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for describing a late penalty awarded to Celtic as "disgusting" in May. The penalty, given for handball, was converted by Kelechi Iheanacho in Celtic's 3-2 win over Motherwell on May 13. McInnes will serve the ban, with one match suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership season.

breaking.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
breaking.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant in response to ongoing attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The potential targets include those located near the capital Tehran. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran and follows reports of air defences being activated in Tehran. The US has not specified when it will carry out the threatened strikes.

breaking.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
breaking.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

breaking.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
breaking.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

facts.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
facts.summary: NASA is set to unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility will be showcased in a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will be available for a brief media availability. The event is open to US citizens and lawful permanent residents, with RSVPs due by July 29.

politics.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
politics.summary: The UK's Conservative party has asked HMRC to investigate a £5 million gift to Nigel Farage, questioning whether it should be taxed as compensation for his failed bid to become an MP. The gift was given by crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne, who claims it was unconditional and for personal reasons. Farage has previously stated the money was for personal security costs and a Brexit campaign reward. HMRC will now determine if the gift should be subject to tax.

politics.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
politics.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar has resigned to join Andy Burnham's government, where he will become a minister at the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. He will also be made a peer, a decision that has been criticized by Scottish Green party co-leader Gillian Mackay as "hypocrisy" given his previous stance against the House of Lords. The move has sparked calls for Sarwar to donate his new salary to charity. The consequences of this decision remain to be seen.

politics.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
politics.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham is facing controversy just three days into office, accused of performing U-turns on key policies including raising the income tax threshold and freezing private sector rents. The prime minister has clarified that raising the income tax threshold remains an option, despite earlier reports suggesting it was ruled out. Burnham's unscripted comments have sparked headaches for his team, with critics questioning his approach to policy-making. His office will now need to navigate the fallout from these minor controversies.

tech.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
tech.summary: A 15-year-old Florida plaintiff has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, claiming the company's social media platforms caused his addiction and harm. The case was set to go before a Los Angeles jury next week. Meta denies the claims, stating they never held up in court. The teenager had previously settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube.

tech.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
tech.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

tech.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
tech.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

crisis.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
crisis.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
crisis.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report targets ultra-processed foods, citing sugar and refined carbohydrates as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee recommends a new approach to food policy, shifting focus from "high in fat, salt or sugar" to specific nutrient targets. This change aims to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for healthier products.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 out of 183 tested towers have confirmed positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to test the remaining towers, with results expected in the coming week. The number of new cases has remained steady, with no new symptoms reported in over 10 days.
